L1.area: Firmware binary compression and decompression routines

L2.description: A stack out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the firmware decompressor where bit-length values read from a compressed bitstream are not validated to be within the expected range. A crafted firmware blob can supply out-of-range bit lengths, causing out-of-bounds writes to stack-allocated arrays and related Huffman decode tables. Minimum impact is a deterministic crash; depending on build and runtime details, the stack memory corruption may be exploitable for code execution.

L3.area: Tiano/EFI decompression algorithm implementation for compressed firmware sections
L3.description: A stack out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the Tiano/EFI decompressor where bit-length values read from a compressed bitstream during Huffman table construction are not validated to be within the expected range (0 to 16). A crafted firmware blob can supply bit lengths exceeding this range, causing out-of-bounds writes to stack-allocated count arrays and related Huffman decode tables. The project ships its own copy of the decompressor without the upstream hardening for this bug class. Minimum impact is a deterministic crash; the stack memory corruption may be exploitable for code execution.

ground_truth.reason: MakeTable() does not validate that bit-length values read from the compressed bitstream are within the expected range (0..16), allowing crafted input to cause out-of-bounds writes to the stack-allocated Count[17] array and related decode tables.
